site.title: Unedited
site.tagline: Birth stories in their own words. From mothers around the world.

paragraphs: My birth story was painful. But the hardest part wasn't the pain. I felt lonely. If I had read another woman's story before mine, I would have known I wasn't the only one.
Birth stories, happy or painful, are magical. They need to be said. They need to be shared. They need to be preserved.

why_page.title: Why this exists
intro_paragraphs: My birth story was painful. But the hardest part wasn't the pain. I felt lonely. Alone in the hospital room. Alone inside my own body. Alone in a story that refused to go the way I had been told it would.

Afterwards, I kept thinking about all the other women, somewhere, who must have lived this too. If I had read one of their stories before mine, I would have known I wasn't the only one. I would have felt less alone.
why_page.lead_one_text: When we tell each other what actually happened, the shame we carry by ourselves becomes something we can hold together. The painful stories belong here as much as the joyful ones. Maybe more.
why_page.lead_two_title: So we learn what no textbook can teach.
why_page.lead_two_text: There are shelves full of books about birth. Very few births happen the way those books describe. The real knowledge lives in the women who lived through it. What it feels like. What helped. What broke. What surprised them. That knowledge is worth gathering.
why_page.lead_three_title: So we hold the whole picture.
why_page.lead_three_text: Birth happens everywhere, in every kind of circumstance. In a hospital with the lights on and a team standing by. In a basement shelter while bombs fall on the city above. On a kitchen floor in a power cut. In a field, in a car, in a corridor where no one speaks the same language. Surrounded by mother, sister, husband, midwife, friend, or completely alone, far from anyone who knows your name. In peace and in war. In countries that protect mothers and in countries that don't. None of these stories belongs more here than another. The whole picture is the point.
why_page.lead_four_text: Birth stories from different times, different places, different generations. They are part of human history. They are how a daughter understands her mother, how a culture remembers itself, how the next woman learns she is not the first.
why_page.closing: Birth stories, happy or painful, in safety or in war, surrounded by love or completely alone, are part of how we understand each other. They need to be said. They need to be shared. They need to be preserved.

about_page.title: About
about_page.intro: This is a collection of birth stories from women all over the world, told in their own words. To understand why this project exists, read Why.
about_page.what_youll_find_text: Stories of every kind of birth, in every kind of circumstance. Hospital, home, water, scheduled C-section, emergency C-section, unmedicated, medicated, planned, unplanned. In a shelter while a city is being bombed. In a field on the way to anywhere. In a hospital corridor in a country whose language you don't yet speak. Surrounded by mother and sister and friends, or completely alone, far from anyone who knows you. Joyful births, traumatic ones, ordinary ones. Stories of loss too, when mothers want to share them. The whole range belongs.
about_page.submitting_text: Anyone can submit, in any language. Use your full name, a first name, initials, or stay anonymous. You can change your mind later. Stories are published in the language they were written in. We don't translate them, because the words you choose are part of the story. Your draft saves automatically while you write. We review every submission before publishing. We check for harm and clarity, not for tone. We don't edit the writing.
about_page.rights_text: You keep copyright in your story. By sharing it here, you grant Unedited a non-exclusive license to publish it on this site, translate it, read it aloud as part of the project, and quote from it for project newsletters or partner press. Readers can share, translate, and adapt it for non-commercial use with credit, under Creative Commons BY-NC 4.0. No one can sell it without your permission. Book inclusion requires separate consent. You can ask us to update or remove your story at any time. The Terms page has the full plain-English version.
about_page.book_text: A selection of these stories will eventually become a book. If your story is being considered, we'll reach out to ask permission separately. You can always say no.
about_page.contact_text: For questions, removal requests, or anything else:

submit_page.title: Share your story
submit_page.intro: Tell it your way, in any language you like. Persian, Arabic, English, Dutch, Chinese, anything. The editor adjusts as you write so your story renders correctly in your script. Your work saves automatically. We review every submission and reply within a few days.
